details: enterprise architecture strategy and AI-augmented engineering in regulated clinical trial environments (FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance context)

AI-augmented engineering since 2023: multi-agent orchestration (CrewAI), RAG systems (LangChain), Model Context Protocol (MCP), workflow automation (n8n), and AI pair programming with Claude Code (since Aug 2024)
enterprise-scale test automation strategy and architecture (Python, Java, Ruby, .NET/C#)
cloud-native and DevOps practices: AWS, CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Packer), containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
performance, security testing, and ethical hacking integrated into test strategy
data analytics dashboards (Dash, Pandas) for actionable quality insights
consultancy, mentoring, and knowledge transfer across engineering teams
clear technical communication using ASR (Architecturally Significant Requirements)

short_intro.intro: I hold a bachelor’s degree in Business Information Systems (2008), ISTQB Foundation certification (2009), and a master’s degree in Management Information Systems (2010).

work.name: Embarcadero
work.summary:
Starting my career in testing as a QA Engineer. Here I learned how to design, develop, and execute tests across common and uncommon use cases, including performance, stress, and stability testing to ensure software quality. At Embarcadero I learned Delphi as the primary language to automate ERStudio Data Architect.
In 2009 I obtained the ISTQB Certification (Foundation Level).

work.name: OSF Global Services
work.summary: After two years in the field, I continued working at OSF Global Services (now OSF Digital). Here I worked extensively with Ruby to implement foundational frameworks for testing web applications for different customers.

work.name: OSF Global Services
work.summary: I love Ruby as a programming language, so I worked as a Ruby on Rails developer for ~1.5 years. Here I began to apply BDD (Behavior‑Driven Development) to implement and deliver a web application similar to Dropbox.

work.name: OSF Global Services
work.summary:
Multiple outsourced projects required automation to accelerate the development cycle. In 2012 I became a QA Team Lead, managing more than four projects simultaneously.
I served as a key point for all QA aspects of releases, providing formal feedback based on best practices. For automation I used Ruby as the primary language.
Coaching, solving work problems, and delivering internal training—primarily on automation—were part of the role.

work.name: Ness
work.summary:
New company, new opportunities ahead!
Here I developed and implemented tests in Java for a large customer (Alfresco, now Hyland). Their powerful, highly scalable enterprise content management (ECM) solution needed robust testing to validate different configurations, integrations with Office, Google Docs, and Salesforce, deployment scenarios, and SDK compatibility across versions.
As a Senior Automation Engineer I maintained and modernized thousands of legacy tests that were sometimes brittle, took a long time to complete, or were implemented using various anti‑patterns.

work.name: Ness
work.summary:
Same company, same Alfresco client, similar testing issues. A need for a new tool arose, so I took the challenging position of QA Automation Architect to build TAS.
In this role I provided technical leadership and strategic direction for automation testing and contributed to implementing and adopting CMMI (Capability Maturity Model Integration) processes within Ness Iasi.
I worked closely with the solutioning team to define and implement POCs for testing challenges, produced cost/benefit analyses, identified resource requirements, defined project estimates, and authored technical specifications.
I served as the primary point of contact in Romania for Test Automation.

work.name: Signant Health
work.summary:
Before the global pandemic I joined Signant Health to continue my path as a Test Automation Architect in a different, exciting industry.
Signant enables clinical trials across traditional, virtual, and hybrid designs and helped Pfizer develop the COVID‑19 vaccine in record time through technology and innovation.
achievements: Built Analytics Dashboard (Dash, Pandas) to analyze legacy test results in real time, provide high‑level overviews (pass rate, duration), cluster defects, compare runs, and identify flaky tests
Revived performance testing by building a tool capable of simulating millions of users to load test applications (AWS, deployed with Terraform)
Migrated jobs from legacy Jenkins to the latest version using Pipeline as Code
Implemented Disaster Recovery tests to validate system resilience and recovery from disasters
fast reliable feedback on regression tests -> reduce duration from 13 hours to 3 hours, increase passing percentage to 99%
Built a Python package for integrating tests with Jira XRAY: automatic test case generation and execution status updates
Enhanced Docker images used in tests, reducing size from 4GB to 600MB—cutting download costs and enabling faster feedback
Reduced technical debt: migrated code and pipelines from a legacy source control system to the current platform
AWS Lift & Shift: moved workloads from in‑house infrastructure to the cloud
Built an automated mechanism to distinguish product bugs from environment issues, reducing manual triage time

work.name: Signant Health
work.summary: Strategic leadership role overseeing automation architecture, framework evolution, and team development.
achievements: Defined comprehensive automation strategies enabling earlier and more reliable regression testing
Developed and applied frameworks addressing functionality, performance, scalability, and maintainability
Collaborated with Software Architects to improve test automation hardware and system architecture
Elevated team capabilities through education, mentorship, and rigorous code review practices
Established and enforced quality standards for test automation implementations

work.name: Signant Health
work.summary: Leading enterprise architecture strategy for a clinical trial technology platform: defining target enterprise architecture, driving modernization initiatives, and pioneering AI‑augmented testing strategies (LLMs, RAG, multi‑agent systems, MCP).
achievements: Designed and implemented an EOL-notifier system leveraging advanced LLM capabilities
Built Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions with LangChain for enterprise knowledge management
Deployed multi-agent automation workflows using CrewAI for collaborative AI operations
Architected and implemented Model Context Protocol (MCP) solutions with custom Python libraries supporting OAuth and basic authentication
Implemented n8n-based internal workflows enabling seamless AI‑human collaboration and process optimization
Integrated Snyk to enhance enterprise security posture with continuous vulnerability scanning
Led gap analysis between current and target architectures; authored comprehensive transition plans
Ensured cross-product technical integration and adherence to enterprise standards
Supported modernization of legacy products to modern software patterns and deployment infrastructure
Provided enterprise architecture guidance and coordination to multiple engineering teams
Identified opportunities for shared services and redundancy reduction across the enterprise
Supported team growth by identifying skill gaps and creating learning opportunities

awards.review:
For the huge impact on TrialConsent Q&A. Outstanding job with creating Disaster Recovery test, performance tests, migration of test automation to AWS and Python3 upgrade. You act like a real enabler for the Trial Consent Team and your work is invaluable for us!
Tudo Cozac (Director of R&D)

Paul, your work on Regression and Testing Architecture is outstanding and has huge impact on Trial Consent. For amazing work on CI pipeline migration. For being always present, always helping, always having the solution for everything. For excellent improvement on precondition data creation, for overall QA team guidance (and not only).
Tudo Cozac (Director of R&D)

Paul, your involvement into the NRF framework will surely make it a great success. Thanks!
(Director R&D Romania)

Great work with L&S Production cut-over testing. Well prepared testing and professionally done work. Thank you!
(VP Testing Service)

certifications.description: Green belt for developers that specialize in Web Application Security Testing.

Security Yellow Belt gives developers an in-depth understanding of the principles, tools, and processes that will keep your products secure.
Security White Belt provides a basic security vocabulary and a solid foundation that will improve communication and prioritize security in your thinking.

Productivity strategies you can start using today and the best tools that will help you become super productive
Learn Penetration Testing from scratch to become a bug bounty hunter and web security tester
